episode_name: Mogadishu urban battle and crash-site rescue corridor
geographic_scope: Central Mogadishu objective area and crash-site rescue corridor
theater_description: Immediate urban area around the Olympic Hotel objective, the two Black Hawk crash sites, and the ground-rescue route fought over during 3-4 October 1993.
geometry_method_note: The episode is narrowed to the battle and rescue corridor only; the broader city population is treated as contextual and unusable as a civilian exposure denominator.
military_area_note: Reliable combatant exposure exists only for selected U.S. assault elements and a broader special operations task force context, not for all engaged forces on both sides.
civilian_area_note: No defensible battle-bounded civilian population-at-risk denominator was found; whole-city population numbers are retained only as context and excluded from core estimates.
split_reason: The case is a single short, tightly bounded urban battle; there is no reason to subdivide it further without creating weaker pseudo-episodes.
quality_note: U.S. battle deaths and battle dates are clear, but Somali dead are reported as a mixed civilian/fighter total and no battle-bounded civilian or full combatant exposure denominator is supportable.
